candidate_summary: AINU-INAU-TIER-STACK: tier-K (K in 1..5) stacking of identical atomic instructions in the system prompt as a magnitude/priority signal, generalising Leviathan 2512.14982's K=2.

results_analysis.title: Prompt Repetition Improves Non-Reasoning LLMs (Leviathan Google Research 2025/12)
results_analysis.reason: Exact twin: Leviathan establishes K=2 stacked-identical-prompt mechanism; candidate explicitly generalises to K in 1..5. Functional and semantic both far above 0.7.

results_analysis.title: The Instruction Hierarchy: Training LLMs to Prioritize Privileged Instructions (OpenAI Wallace 2024)
results_analysis.reason: Title contains 'Instruction' and 'Prioritize' overlapping with 'atomic-instruction multiplicity' and 'prompt priority' content words; 5-tier privilege hierarchy is functionally equivalent to K in 1..5 priority encoding.

results_analysis.title: Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy in LLM Agents (2026/04)
results_analysis.reason: Title literally says 'Many-Tier Instruction Hierarchy' matching 'instruction-tier-stack' and 'tier-count' content words; semantic and functional both above 0.7.

results_analysis.title: Dead simple prompt repetition boosts accuracy 76% (VentureBeat)
results_analysis.reason: Industry coverage of identical Leviathan mechanism; multiple kw matches plus sem and func above 0.7.

results_analysis.title: Prompt Repetition: 67% accuracy gain at zero latency cost
results_analysis.reason: Same Leviathan double-prompting mechanism; 'Prompt Repetition' literally matches content-word substrings.

results_analysis.title: PromptReps: Representation via Repeated Prompts (Emergent Mind)
results_analysis.reason: K-repeated prompt mechanism for representations; functionally same K-stack construction.

results_analysis.title: Double Prompting: How Prompt Repetition Improves LLM Accuracy
results_analysis.reason: Alias of Leviathan double-prompting effect; same K=2 stack mechanism.

results_analysis.title: Ultimate Guide to Prompt Engineering 2026 (Lakera)
results_analysis.reason: Single keyword match (kw fails), but sem 0.72 and func 0.72 both meet 0.7 threshold; documents priority-tier instruction stacking, the exact recombined ingredient cited in the candidate.

verdict_sentence: All 8 results clear the OR-threshold (Leviathan 2512.14982 is a near-exact twin at sem 0.92 / func 0.94 with K=2, generalised by candidate to K in 1..5; multiple corroborating prompt-repetition and instruction-hierarchy papers further saturate the threshold), so the candidate is decisively non-novel and FAILS, matching the primary agent's FAIL verdict.
